page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m1.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. This instance features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 8 vCPUs. It is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps under a KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates that this instance delivers entry-level compute and memory performance. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth read speeds are similarly positioned in the bottom 25%, though large-block write bandwidth reaches average levels. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show poor performance, placing in the bottom 10%. However, the instance achieves average performance in Passmark CPU and extended instruction tests, as well as Redis SET operations and small-scale (135M) LLM inference tasks. Medium-scale (7B) LLM inference drops to the bottom 10% tier.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.u0.c1m1.2xlarge offers a high vCPU count relative to its memory capacity, making it a specialized option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is the highly constrained 8.0 GB memory limit across 8 vCPUs, which restricts its suitability for memory-intensive applications or large databases. This instance is best suited for lightweight multi-threaded processing, basic web serving, Redis caching with small datasets, and utility computing tasks that benefit from dedicated cores but have low memory demands.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m1.2xlarge is an x86_64 server instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 8.0 GB of system memory. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this KVM-based instance operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results place its single-core and multi-core CPU performance largely in the bottom 25% tier, with memory read bandwidth and text processing also showing weak performance. However, it achieves average marks in Passmark CPU tests, Redis SET operations, and small-model LLM inference. The 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio presents a clear tradeoff, limiting the instance to memory-light workloads. It is best suited for basic multi-threaded utility tasks, lightweight web serving, and small-scale caching operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m1.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM. It offers entry-level compute performance and is suited for lightweight, memory-constrained utility workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m1.2xlarge features 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering entry-level compute for memory-light utility workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core x86 server configured with minimal memory for lightweight, CPU-driven utility workloads and basic caching.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory (1.0 GB per core)
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average performance in Redis SET operations and small LLM inference
Weak single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark results
Suited for lightweight multi-threaded utility tasks and small-scale caching
